response:
In C# it is possible to measure the time taken for a UDP packet to reach a server  
but it requires some additional steps  
UDP does not guarantee delivery or timing information  
so you must use a different approach  

You can send a UDP packet and then wait for a response  
but this is not always reliable  
Alternatively you can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient class  
and use the SendAsync method to send the packet  
then use a Stopwatch to measure the time between sending and receiving  

You can also use the Ping class but it measures round trip time  
not one way time  
To measure one way time you must implement your own logic  

You can use the DateTime class to record the time before sending the packet  
and then record the time when you receive a response  
then calculate the difference between the two times  

This method is not always accurate  
because the server may not respond immediately  
or may not respond at all  
But it can give an approximate idea of the time taken  

You can also use the NetworkInformation class  
but it does not provide one way time information  

In summary to measure the time taken for a UDP packet to reach a server  
you must send the packet and then wait for a response  
and then calculate the time difference between sending and receiving  
This is the only way to get one way time information in C#
